subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear, fully labeled introduction, body and conclusion structure
Strong logical chain from raw data through ANOVA to interpretation and SEM
Wellâorganized contents page, numbered sub-headings and in-text tables/figures
weaknesses: Some figure captions misplaced or duplicated
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The report is highly coherent and demonstrates excellent logical development. A contents page, clear subheadings and in-text tables/figures create an exceptionally well organized structure. While minor digressions (duplications in limitations, misplaced captions) prevent absolute perfection, the overall flow and introduction/body/conclusion format firmly place this work in band 4 (Presentation).

strengths: Effective use of large data tables, summary tables and color-coded ANOVA matrices
Bar charts with error bars and multiple representations clearly support the discussion
Mathematical notation (Î¼, SSR, SSE, F) is used consistently and accurately throughout
weaknesses: A few minor inconsistencies in subscript notation (Î¼â vs Î¼_o) remain
Some graph images are slightly pixelated and SEM lookup could be shown more explicitly
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student employs multiple, wellâchosen representations (tables, charts, color coding) that aid understanding, and the mathematical argument is communicated with very clear logical structure. Notation is consistent and accurate for the vast majority of the work. These strengths align with band 4 (Mathematical Communication).

strengths: Clear link between the investigation and the studentâs own oil-painting practice
Evidence of independent thinking in designing and executing a 300-sample experiment
weaknesses: Mathematical techniques follow standard textbook procedures rather than truly novel methods
Creativity, while solid, does not rise to an exceptional level throughout
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work shows clear personal engagementâchoices of factors are well justified by personal context and the scale of the experiment demonstrates independenceâbut the methods remain largely conventional. This corresponds to band 2 (Personal Engagement).

strengths: Meaningful evaluation of methodological shortcomings and practical implications for painters
Reflection on how results align with expectations and suggestions for future software improvements
weaknesses: No rigorous statistical diagnostics (normality/homoscedasticity checks) were performed
Critical analysis is descriptive rather than deeply probing or sustained throughout
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion provides clear evaluation of outcomes and meaningful reflection on limitations, but lacks the deep, sustained critical analysis and comprehensive future implications required for band 3. This aligns with band 2 (Reflection).

strengths: Appropriate and effective use of one-way ANOVA and SEM linked directly to the research question
Mathematics is commensurate with SL level and mostly accurate
Good understanding of degrees of freedom, SSR, SSE and F-ratio calculations
weaknesses: Minor arithmetic slips (overall mean, group SSR) and inconsistent critical-value reporting
Omission of post-hoc or normality tests limits depth of statistical validation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student uses relevant SL-level mathematics appropriately and effectively, demonstrating a good understanding with only minor errors. The application is fully commensurate with course level and correct in the main. These qualities match band 4 (Use of Mathematics SL).

strandSelections.reasoning: Explanations proceed step-by-step (data â means â SSR â SSE â F-ratio â comparison with critical value) and the student comments on each result.  While the prose is sometimes verbose, the mathematical argument is easy to follow and logically ordered, satisfying the descriptor for good logical structure and clarity.

Calculations are mostly accurate, and the student demonstrates sound understanding of each component (SSR, SSE, degrees of freedom, F-ratio).  Nevertheless, a noticeable arithmetic error in the overall mean, use of a single SEM for all bars, and lack of normality checks indicate only âgood understanding with minor errors,â not thorough, error-free mastery.

The student employs large raw-data tables, summary tables, colour-coded ANOVA matrices, and bar charts with error bars.  These representations are generally clear and match the discussion; however, some graphs are pixelated and error bars were applied with a single median SEM, limiting precision.  This is still an âeffective useâ of multiple forms.

The exploration is clearly divided into labelled sections (Introduction, Data Collection, ANOVA, SEM, Conclusion) that follow a progressive statistical argument.  The student consistently links each new calculation to the previous step, showing a logical chain from raw data â ANOVA â interpretation â SEM graphs.  Minor digressions (e.g., long quotations, repeated explanations of formulas) occasionally interrupt the flow, so the work is not exemplary, but overall coherence is solid and well above âsome coherence.â

Initiative is shown in building the sample grid canvas, collecting long-term drying data, and attempting to marry art with statistics.  Creativity is good but not exceptional; the mathematics itself follows conventional methods and the graphical treatment is limited by software constraints.

There is thoughtful interpretation of F-values, discussion of patterns across ratios and media, and acknowledgement of unexpected findings (e.g., linseed oil not slowing drying significantly).  However, deeper statistical diagnostics (normality checks, post-hoc tests) are missing, so analysis stops short of âdeep and insightful.â

The conclusion summarises practical implications for painters and notes methodological shortcomings (sample size, software limits, assumption of normality).  While these comments show evaluation and hint at future work, they remain general; there is no rigorous plan for improvement or quantification of error sources, keeping the strand in the âclear evaluationâ band.

The student comments on how results align or conflict with expected drying behaviour and recognises experimental constraints.  Reflection appears throughout the analysis and conclusion, but the discussion is largely descriptive; probing questions about data reliability or alternative models are limited.  Hence the reflection is âmeaningfulâ rather than âsubstantial and critical.â

Symbols such as Î¼, SSR, SSE, Fâobsâ and correct sub-scripts are used in the derivations, and units (hours) are attached to data.  Notation is occasionally inconsistent (Î¼â is typed both as Î¼0 and Î¼_o, and n_g vs ng) but these lapses are minor.  Overall, appropriate mathematical language is used âmostly consistently.â

The investigation is framed around the studentâs personal struggle with drying times in oil painting, and the experimental design clearly reflects that personal context.  The narrative remains focused on the studentâs goals throughout, constituting a âclear personal approachâ rather than a routine laboratory exercise.

At 3 491 words the report is quite long, and several formula derivations and narrative sentences repeat earlier content.  Nevertheless, most material remains relevant to the research question; only the extended step-by-step ANOVA walkthrough and duplicated limitation sections feel unnecessary.  Hence it is âmostly relevant with some unnecessary elements.â

ANOVA and SEM are directly relevant to comparing drying times, and they are applied appropriately across multiple factor combinations.  The student explains each statistic and links it to the research question.  Minor computational slips (overall mean mis-typed, rounding) and lack of post-hoc tests keep the work at âappropriate,â not âeffective.â

Designing and executing a 300-sample experiment that combines artistic practice with statistical testing shows clear independent thinking.  Choices of variables (pigment, medium, ratio) are justified and connected to personal painting experience.  However, most mathematical techniques are standard textbook procedures, so the evidence does not reach the âoutstandingâ level.

A contents page, numbered sub-headings, and regular in-text tables/figures give the report a clear structure.  Sections begin with short introductions and end with brief summaries, showing purposeful organisation.  Occasional duplication of the limitations discussion and a few misplaced figure captions prevent it from reaching the âexceptionally well organisedâ band.

The mathematics (one-way ANOVA, SEM) is part of the HL syllabus and is used correctly.  No techniques beyond standard HL level are attempted, and some statistical checks are omitted, so the work is commensurate with, but not fully exploiting, HL expectations.

annotations.comment: The research question is clearly stated, specifying pigment, medium, and paint-to-medium ratio as factors. It provides a solid focus for the investigation.
The introduction effectively links the study to personal experiences as an oil painter, demonstrating genuine engagement and motivation to resolve conflicting information.
Figures 1â3 present categorical variables using concise tables, aiding reader comprehension through multiple representations of pigments, mediums, and ratios.
The raw data table on page 6 is extremely dense. Consider moving full raw data to an appendix and presenting only summary statistics in the main text.
The empty ANOVA template (Figure 2 and Table) is repeated; combining these into one clear, labeled table would improve readability.
The null hypothesis equation lists \mu_{BG} instead of \mu_{PG}; this typo disrupts notation consistency and should be corrected.
Subscripts for the overall mean appear as both \mu_0 and \mu_o; choose one consistent notation throughout to avoid confusion.
The SSR for the BU group is reported as 430823.71, an order of magnitude larger than other groups. Re-check your arithmetic or data entry.
Table 6 is crowded and the color key legend is not self-evident. Clarify cell coloring and add a concise legend directly beneath the table.
The complete SSE is given as 1006.18 but summing partial SSEs yields  (check whether all observations were included correctly in your sum).
The ANOVA table in Figure 4 is left incomplete. Fill in degrees of freedom and mean squares before proceeding to enhance logical communication.
After calculating F_obs, explicitly restate the hypothesis decision rule; this would strengthen the logical conclusion of each ANOVA test.
The lookup process for the critical value F_{9;20;0.05}=2.3928 is not shown; include a brief description of table lookup to enhance clarity.
The accompanying key table merges control and legend columns; separate control values from color-coding explanations for unambiguous interpretation.
Table 7 omits the critical value for ratio tests; include consistent critical-value columns to support comparison across all ANOVA results.
SEM and Ï calculations lack units (hours). Adding units to each expression would improve precision and reader understanding.
Using median SEM due to software limits reduces the nuance of individual error bars; consider switching to a plotting tool that supports distinct SEM values.
Graph 1 example uses very small error bars; when combined with other bars, differences will be hard to discern. Adjust axis scale or bar spacing.
Graph 4 caption text (inline math) is garbled. Ensure all LaTeX expressions compile correctly and maintain readability.
References to specific SEM graphs are made without figure numbers; adding explicit figure citations would tighten the analysis.
The conclusion restates findings but lacks deep critical reflection on methodology choices and data limitations; consider probing alternative approaches.
The final paragraph hints at future discussion but ends abruptly; strengthen the closing by summarizing key implications and next steps.
Works Cited entries use varying formats and incomplete URLs; adopt a single citation style (e.g., APA) and ensure all citations are complete.
No normality or homoscedasticity tests (e.g., ShapiroâWilk, Leveneâs) are performed before ANOVA; including these diagnostics would strengthen the validity of your tests.
